evidence: Ascent resonates with physical movement (ontological.material), sensory perception (epistemological.perception), and lived experience (epistemological.experience). It contrasts with falling by requiring contingent conditions (lawfulness.contingent) and has aesthetic value (value_generation.aesthetic). Its opposition to falling introduces a transformative dynamic (symmetry.transformative).

Bagging (as the act of placing items in a bag) resonates most strongly with physical/material aspects (ontological.material), individual scale (scale.individual), and functional utility (value_generation.functional). It weakly connects to triggering a fall (interconnection.catalytic) and predictable outcomes (lawfulness.predictable) when overloaded bags drop. Superficial resonance exists due to indirect causal links between bagging and falling.

The word resonates strongly with perceptual and material aspects of falling (direct observation, physical movement). Moderate scores reflect identification/naming, understanding gravity's role, and relational dynamics between object and force. Highest scores align with predictability and the necessity of gravitational motion.

Declination resonates most strongly with the physical/material layer (ontological.material), the inevitability of gravity (lawfulness.necessary), predictability (lawfulness.predictable), and the core essence of downward motion (resonance.essential). Secondary scores reflect individual-scale actions (scale.individual), sensory perception (epistemological.perception), and lived experience (epistemological.experience).

The strongest scores reflect the physical nature of decline (ontological.material), its direct perception (epistemological.perception), and its essential resonance as a core downward movement. Lower scores acknowledge limited connections to scale beyond individual entities and destructive symmetry in contexts where falling has negative consequences.

The highest scores reflect the physical nature of falling (ontological.material=10), its adherence to gravitational laws (lawfulness.predictable=10, lawfulness.necessary=8), direct observability (epistemological.perception=5), and the core essence of the action (resonance.essential=5). Lower scores relate to individual-scale actions (scale.individual=3) and embodied experience (epistemological.experience=4).

Declivitous directly relates to physical slopes (ontological.material 7) that indicate potential falls (temporal.indication 2). It catalyzes downward motion (interconnection.catalytic 3) and operates predictably (lawfulness.predictable 4). Scores reflect its tangible, structural role in enabling gravity-driven movement without moral or existential implications.

Declivity directly resonates with physical material (10) as a downward slope. Its structural role in enabling falling (resonance.essential 7) and independence (5) align with the atom's gravity-driven motion. Perception (5) and catalytic influence (4) reflect its observable, causal presence in descent scenarios.

Descent strongly resonates with physical/material existence (10), predictability (10), and necessity (8) due to gravity's role. It scores highly in perception (7), identification (5), and experiential embodiment (4), as falling is directly observable and felt. The act's core essence (7) and potential destructiveness (3) also align with the atom's definition.

The word 'downhill' strongly resonates with physical/material reality (ontological.material:10) and the inevitability of gravity-driven motion (lawfulness.predictable:10). It implies a persistent, ongoing process (temporal.continuation:5) and the structural conditions enabling the fall (ontological.structural:5). The necessity of the motion (lawfulness.necessary:5) and its functional description (value_generation.functional:5) further align with the atom's definition. Resonance.essential (5) reflects its core role in defining the direction of the fall.

The word 'downhill' strongly resonates with its physical material form (10) and the essential nature of falling (10). Its predictable nature (8) aligns with gravity's laws. Perception (7) and identification (5) reflect direct sensory recognition and categorization. Individual scale (5) and functional value (5) highlight its practical use as a path or slope.

Downslope strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material), direct perception (epistemological.perception), and catalyzing falls (interconnection.catalytic). Its essential role in the act of falling (resonance.essential) and predictable nature (lawfulness.predictable) are core. Functional utility (value_generation.functional) and independence (interconnection.independent) also score highly.

Droop strongly resonates with material physicality (ontological.material), sensory perception (epistemological.perception), and inevitability under gravity (lawfulness.predictable). It also connects to individual-scale sagging (scale.individual) and aesthetic qualities (value_generation.aesthetic). Scores drop for abstract categories like transformation or ethical value.

Droop resonates strongly with ontological.material (8) as it describes physical sagging. It scores high in lawfulness.predictable (7) since gravity-driven movement is expected. The superficial resonance (6) reflects the visible, surface-level nature of drooping.

The drop cloth directly relates to ACT.fall through its material form (10) and functional purpose (10) of catching falling debris. Its presence indicates anticipation of falling (2) and operates at an individual scale (2). Its structural role (2) and relational ties to falling objects (3) further align with the atom.

The word 'drop curtain' strongly resonates with physical/material existence (ontological.material 10), individual scale (scale.individual 10), and the act of falling as a predictable, deliberate motion (lawfulness.predictable 10). It also connects to perception/identification (epistemological scores 10) and functional value (value_generation.functional 10). The essential resonance (8) and transformative symmetry (5) reflect its role as a definitive endpoint, while aesthetic value (5) acknowledges its symbolic elegance.

The highest scores reflect its physical nature (ontological.material), gravity's predictability (lawfulness.predictable), and core essence of falling (resonance.essential). Perception and functional value are strong due to direct observation and practical applications.

The action 'drop open' strongly resonates with the material aspect (physical movement due to gravity) and the predictable nature of falling. It catalyzes further actions (like items falling out) and involves a transformative change from closed to open. The action is superficially observed and essential to the concept of falling.

The highest scores reflect that 'fall' is a physical (ontological.material), individual-scale action (scale.individual) perceived directly (epistemological.perception) and tied to direct experience (epistemological.experience). It operates predictably under gravity (lawfulness.predictable) and touches the core essence of motion (resonance.essential). Lower scores acknowledge weak connections to aesthetic value (e.g., falling leaves) and transformative effects (e.g., environmental change after a fall).

The word 'fall' (v) strongly resonates with temporal.influence (ongoing motion), scale.individual (physical objects), ontological.material (physicality), interconnection.independent (autonomous motion), resonance.structural (gravity's law), and lawfulness.predictable/necessary (inevitable due to gravity). Weak connections exist in temporal.continuation (ongoing drop), epistemological.experience (embodied sensation), and interconnection.chained (potential cascades).

The highest scores reflect its physical, observable nature (material, perception), its individual scale, and gravitational predictability. The semantic score arises from combining 'fall' and 'open,' while functional value may apply if the action serves a purpose (e.g., a book landing open on a page).

The verb 'flag' strongly resonates with material physicality (ontological.material), individual-scale actions (scale.individual), and predictable processes (lawfulness.predictable). It also aligns with sensory perception (epistemological.perception) and naming the action (epistemological.identification). The drooping motion (flagging) is an early indicator (temporal.indication) and part of a causal chain (interconnection.chained) leading to falling. Scores drop to 0 for abstract or systemic categories.

Pratfall strongly resonates with scale.individual (10) and ontological.material (10) as it is a physical, personal action. It scores highly in epistemological.identification (8) due to its specific lexical meaning and value_generation.aesthetic (8) due to its comedic connotation. Resonance.superficial (7) reflects its surface-level, often humorous manifestation. Lower scores reflect its lack of systemic or existential depth.

Sag as a noun in the context of physical sinking resonates most with temporal continuation (ongoing drooping), scale.individual (common in physical objects), ontological.material (physical drooping), and lawfulness.predictable (gravity-driven). Scores reflect its gradual, persistent nature and material focus, avoiding over-scoring in abstract or large-scale categories.

Sag strongly resonates with physical (ontological.material), individual scale (scale.individual), and direct perception (epistemological.perception). It is tied to predictable gravity-driven motion (lawfulness.predictable) and manifests as both a superficial visible change (resonance.superficial) and an essential aspect of falling (resonance.essential). Scores are minimized elsewhere to prioritize core connections.

Sag down strongly resonates with physical/material reality (ontological.material 10) and individual-scale actions (scale.individual 8). It is fundamentally tied to gravitational necessity (lawfulness.necessary 5) and predictability (lawfulness.predictable 8). Its essential resonance (resonance.essential 5) and structural/relational aspects (ontological.structural 6, ontological.relational 4) reflect how weight and form interact. Perception (7) and embodied experience (5) are key to its understanding.

Slouch strongly resonates with physicality (ontological.material, perception, experience) and individual scale. It weakly indicates a potential fall (temporal.indication) and may catalyze a fall (symmetry.destructive). Its structural aspects (ontological.structural, resonance.structural) tie to posture's relation to downward movement.

The word 'sloucher' strongly resonates with scale.individual (7) and ontological.material (7) because it refers to a person's physical posture. It connects to interconnection.catalytic (6) as poor posture can trigger falls. Scores for epistemological.identification (5) and ontological.semantic (5) reflect the term's direct naming and meaning. Lower scores reflect weak or indirect ties to other slots.

The highest scores reflect 'spill's physical nature (ontological.material), its direct observability (epistemological.perception), and its transient, surface-level manifestation (resonance.superficial). Scores are low elsewhere due to the word's narrow contextual focus on the act of falling/movement downward.

Steep resonates most strongly with physical materiality (slope/angle), catalyzing falls through gravity. Scores peak in material ontology (5), predictable lawfulness (4), and structural resonance (3). Lower scores reflect sensory perception (3), experiential embodiment (3), and contingent causality (3).

The verb 'swag' in the context of falling resonates most strongly with superficial resonance (2), individual scale (2), material ontology (2), and predictable lawfulness (2). These scores reflect its slang usage to describe physical movement (material), its focus on individual action (individual), and the inherent predictability of gravity-driven motion (predictable). Lower scores (1) indicate weak but real connections to identification (e.g., naming the action), creation of a new term, and functional utility in contexts like sports slang.

The verb 'tip' strongly resonates with material reality (ontological.material) and small-scale physical actions (scale.individual). It implies an early stage of falling (temporal.indication) and predictable cause-effect chains (lawfulness.predictable). The action's physical manifestation aligns with sensory perception (epistemological.perception) and practical utility (value_generation.functional). Scores above 2 reflect direct connections to the physical process of falling.

Topple strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material), individual scale (scale.individual), and predictable lawfulness (lawfulness.predictable) due to its physical nature and reliance on gravity. Weaker scores reflect secondary aspects like early signs of falling (temporal.indication) or causing chain reactions (interconnection.chained).

Tumble strongly resonates with ontological.material (physical action), resonance.essential (core essence of falling), and epistemological.experience (embodied knowing). Moderate scores reflect its sensory perception (visual/motion), individual scale, and predictable nature under gravity. Lower scores acknowledge its transient, non-transformative, and non-structural aspects.

Tumble strongly resonates with ontological.material (7) as it's a physical action. Essential resonance (7) captures its core essence of falling. Predictable lawfulness (6) reflects gravity's role. Aesthetic value (4) comes from graceful or chaotic motion. Interconnection.independent (4) and catalytic (3) reflect both solitary and impactful falls.

Wipeout strongly resonates with material, semantic, and perceptual aspects of a sudden fall. It implies destructive force (symmetry.destructive) and operates at an individual scale. Scores for catalytic/chained reflect potential consequences, while emergent/lawfulness highlight its unexpected nature. Aesthetic value arises from its dramatic visual impact.
